title: ficus kunthii
intro: Ficus kunthii, also known as the Indian banyan or strangler fig, is a species of fig tree native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of Ficus kunthii include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as acting as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. In traditional medicine, Ficus kunthii is often prepared as a paste or decoction, and can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, ointments, and infusions.
